title: apium graveolens var. lusitanicum
intro: Apium graveolens var. lusitanicum, also known as wild celery, is a variety of the Apiaceae family and a type of wild celery that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis, menstrual cramps, and urinary issues. The health benefits of Apium graveolens var. lusitanicum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antiseptic. Wild celery can be consumed as a herbal preparation, such as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
